data.title: SPS Commerce Non-GAAP EPS of $0.92 beats by $0.09, revenue of $163.69M beats by $3.39M
data.description:
• SPS Commerce press release (NASDAQ:SPSC): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.92 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $163.69M (+20.7% Y/Y) beats by $3.39M.
  • Shares -3.13%.

  • Fourth Quarter 2024 Guidance

    • Revenue is expected to be in the range of $168.5 million to $169.5 million, representing 16% to 17% year-over-year growth. Q4 consensus Revenue estimate is $167.57M.
    • Net income per diluted share is expected to be in the range of $0.46 to $0.47, with fully diluted weighted average shares outstanding of 38.1 million shares.
    • Non-GAAP income per diluted share is expected to be in the range of $0.83 to $0.84. Q4 consensus EPS estimate is $0.88.
    • Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be in the range of $48.0 million to $48.7 million.
    • Non-cash, share-based compensation expense is expected to be $12.8 million, depreciation expense is expected to be $5.1 million, and amortization expense is expected to be $7.2 million.

    Fiscal Year 2024 Guidance

    • Revenue is expected to be in the range of $635.4 million to $636.4 million, representing 18% to 19% growth over 2023. FY24 consensus Revenue estimate is $631.31M.
    • Net income per diluted share is expected to be in the range of $2.03 to $2.04, with fully diluted weighted average shares outstanding of 37.9 million shares.
    • Non-GAAP income per diluted share is expected to be in the range of $3.41 to $3.42. FY24 consensus EPS estimate is $3.36.
    • Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be in the range of $185.0 million to $185.7 million, representing 17% to 18% growth over 2023.
    • Non-cash, share-based compensation expense is expected to be $55.1 million, depreciation expense is expected to be $19.1 million, and amortization expense is expected to be $22.8 million.

data.title: Healthpeak bumps up low end of FFO guidance as Q3 results feature strong leasing
data.description:
Healthpeak Properties (NYSE:DOC) stock gained 1.2% in Thursday after-hours trading after lifting the midpoint of its 2024 guidance for funds from operations and delivering Q3 results that featured continued strong momentum in leasing activity.

The healthcare REIT now expects 2024 FFO per share, as adjusted, of $1.79-$1.81, compared with the prior view of $1.77-$1.81 and the $1.79 average analyst estimate.

Total merger-combined same-store cash (adjusted) net operating income growth is expected to be 3.5%-4.5% vs. 2.75%-4.25% in the previous target.

Healthpeak (DOC), which merged with Physicians Realty Trust earlier this year, expects to achieve at least $50M of merger-related synergies in 2024, up from $45M in the previous guidance issued in July, driven by property management internalization.

Q3 FFO per share, as adjusted, of $0.44, matching the consensus, ticked down from $0.45 in both Q2 and the year-earlier quarter.

Revenue of $700.4M, surpassing the $684.5M expected, advanced from $695.5M in Q2 and $556.2M a year before.

Costs and expenses slipped to $665.2M from $666.2M in the previous quarter and climbed from $490.4M in Q3 2023.

During the quarter ended Sept. 30 and through Oct. 24, Healthpeak (DOC) executed lab lease agreements totaling 733K square feet. Year-to-date through Oct. 24, the company has executed 1.7M square feet of lab leases with an additional 575K square feet under signed letters of intent.

Conference call on Oct. 25 at 8:00 a.m. ET.

data.title: Morgan Stanley appoints Ted Pick for added role of chairman in 2025
data.description:
• Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) said Ted Pick will become chairman, in addition to his current CEO role, on Jan. 1, 2025, succeeding James Gorman, who will retire from the company.
  • "It is time to hand the reins to the next generation of exceptional talent at Morgan Stanley," said Gorman, who led the company for the past 15 years.
  • Earlier this week, Gorman was picked to be chairman of Disney (DIS) as of Jan. 2.

data.title: Dexcom stock tumbles 14% post-market amid Q3 earnings report
data.description:
Shares of Dexcom (NASDAQ:DXCM) fell 14% in post-market trading Thursday after the company released its Q3 earnings report which showed only modest topline growth over last year’s quarter.

Dexcom reported that revenue rose 2%, year-over-year, on a reported basis to $994M, with U.S. revenue slipping 2%. Adjusted earnings per share were $0.45, according to a statement.

Analysts, on average, were expecting adjusted EPS of $0.43 on revenue of $991.2M.

Dexcom also reiterated its 2024 guidance of revenue of between $4B and $4.05B. Analysts, on average, are expecting $4.01B.
